summary: PR #2323 replaced two decomp placeholders with C implementations for mnname IsNameUnique and toy un_80305D00, and updated the IsNameUnique header prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot). The automated report in the issue comments records two new 100% matches totaling +316 matched bytes. The PR body appears unrelated to the actual diff, describing grIceMt_801F91EC and grKongo_801D8078 instead of the changed mn/ty functions.

key_files.role: Implemented IsNameUnique as a bounded scan over name slots, using IsNameValid, GetPersistentNameData, and CompareNameStrings.
evidence_refs: issue_comment report: main/melee/mn/mnname IsNameUnique +140 bytes 0.00% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Updated IsNameUnique declaration from unknown return/params to bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot).
Implemented un_80305D00 as a loop over four controller pad statuses, returning the first nml_stickX value whose absolute value exceeds 0.1F or the final sampled value.
evidence_refs: issue_comment report: main/melee/ty/toy un_80305D00 +176 bytes 0.00% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: Replaced the /// #IsNameUnique placeholder with a bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot) implementation that loops i from 0 to 0x77, checks IsNameValid(i & 0xFF), obtains GetPersistentNameData((u8) i)->namedata when valid, breaks on NULL namedata, and returns true when CompareNameStrings(namedata, slot) == 0.
Changed the IsNameUnique prototype in mnname.h from UNK_RET IsNameUnique(UNK_PARAMS) to bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot).

Replaced the /// #un_80305D00 placeholder with a float un_80305D00(void) implementation that scans HSD_PadCopyStatus[0..3].nml_stickX, computes an absolute-value threshold check using 0.1F, breaks when exceeded, and returns the stored sampled stick value.
The automated GALE01 report recorded two new 100.00% matches: un_80305D00 in main/melee/ty/toy (+176 bytes) and IsNameUnique in main/melee/mn/mnname (+140 bytes).

smart_moves.text: The PR updated the owning header prototype after recovering the IsNameUnique body, removing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for that symbol.
Both recovered functions use compact bounded for-loops instead of leaving placeholder or repeated generated-looking code.
The toy function uses the typed HSD_PadCopyStatus array and nml_stickX field rather than raw pointer arithmetic for pad data access.

decomp_lessons.text: When a matched function body proves a signature, update the owning header from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to the recovered return and parameter types in the same focused change.

Small scan functions can match cleanly as natural bounded loops: IsNameUnique uses a for loop over 0x78 possible entries with an early break on NULL data, and un_80305D00 uses a for loop over four pad slots with an early threshold break.

naming_conventions.text: Recovered semantic names already present in the symbol list or surrounding code were preserved: IsNameUnique, IsNameValid, GetPersistentNameData, CompareNameStrings, and namedata.
The unknown-address-style name un_80305D00 remained unchanged despite body recovery, consistent with not inventing semantics beyond available evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used explicit casts to u8 in index-based accesses for matching-sensitive calls and array indexing: IsNameUnique passes (u8) i to GetPersistentNameData, while un_80305D00 indexes HSD_PadCopyStatus[(u8) i].
In un_80305D00, retained both ret and val locals so the function can check the absolute value of val while returning the original signed stick value sampled before negation.
Used a ternary in IsNameUnique to assign either NULL or namedata based on IsNameValid before a shared NULL break check.

review_feedback.text: No review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.
The only loaded issue comment was an automated GALE01 match report, not human review feedback.

follow_up_queries: PR body mismatch grIceMt_801F91EC grKongo_801D8078 PR 2323

notes: Diff and changed-file metadata strongly support the actual code and header changes.
The issue comment report supports that IsNameUnique and un_80305D00 were newly matched at 100.00%.

The PR body is inconsistent with the title and diff: it describes grIceMt_801F91EC and grKongo_801D8078 in stage files that were not changed in this slice. Those body claims were not treated as facts about this PR's actual code changes.
No human review comments or reviewer intent were available.
No local build, objdiff, or checkdiff logs beyond the loaded automated GALE01 report were available.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover header prototypes with matched bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2323 is a compact example of updating an owning header prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to the recovered signature when the function body is decompiled: IsNameUnique became bool IsNameUnique(s32 slot) in both mnname.c and mnname.h.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use typed pad status access for controller scan helpers
accepted_candidate_records.text: un_80305D00 matched as a four-entry loop over HSD_PadCopyStatus[(u8) i].nml_stickX, preserving typed field access and a separate returned sample value while using an absolute-value threshold for the break condition.

source_update_candidates.title: Flag PR #2323 body/diff mismatch in past-PR corpus
source_update_candidates.text: The loaded PR body for #2323 describes grIceMt_801F91EC in gricemt.c and grKongo_801D8078 in grkongo.c, but the title, changed files, diff, and automated match report all concern IsNameUnique in mnname and un_80305D00 in toy. Curator may want to annotate the source corpus record so downstream consumers do not treat the PR body summary as evidence for this slice's changed files.
source_update_candidates.reason: Prevent unrelated PR-body text from being promoted as facts about the changed mn/ty functions.

rejection_notes: Do not record the PR body's Icicle Mountain or Kongo Jungle function descriptions as facts for PR #2323's actual changed files; they are unsupported by the diff and changed-file metadata in this slice.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; the loaded slice has no review comments.
Do not generalize the exact local variable choices ret/val/i into a global style rule; they are only evidence for this matched function.
